headline: Food Safety Violations at 34% of Inspected Premises in 2025
context: Joint MOH-local authority inspections found food safety violations at over one-third of food establishments checked.

cards.big: 34% of food premises failed safety inspections out of 128,000 establishments checked in 2025
cards.sub: Violations include improper storage contamination risks and expired ingredients

cards.big: Food poisoning cases totaled 38,000 in 2025 with 28 deaths from contaminated food
cards.sub: Actual cases estimated at 3-5 times reported figures due to underreporting

cards.big: Average fine for food safety violations is RM1,500 far below the RM100,000 maximum
cards.sub: Low penalties provide insufficient deterrent for repeat offenders

cards.big: South Korea's real-time food safety grading system reduced violations by 42% since 2019
cards.sub: Public display of hygiene ratings creates market incentive for compliance

cards.big: Inspecting 128,000 premises with fines averaging RM1,500 is regulation theater not food safety
Should Malaysia mandate public display of food safety grades at all food establishments
